title: Terrestrial and marine protected areas (% of total territorial area)
definition: Terrestrial protected areas are totally or partially protected areas of at least 1,000 hectares that are designated by national authorities as scientific reserves with limited public access, national parks, natural monuments, nature reserves or wildlife sanctuaries, protected landscapes, and areas managed mainly for sustainable use. Marine protected areas are areas of intertidal or subtidal terrain--and overlying water and associated flora and fauna and historical and cultural features--that have been reserved by law or other effective means to protect part or all of the enclosed environment. Sites protected under local or provincial law are excluded.

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for protected land and seas.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include New Caledonia, Bhutan, United Kingdom, while the lightest include Eritrea, Somalia, Fed. Rep., Vanuatu; that contrast is the fastest way to read the protected land and seas pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Protected land and seas may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Protected land can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Protected seas can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Environment maps reward separating land cover, exposure, and emissions intensity.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Protected land and seas passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
